title: stachys palustris subsp. ambigua
intro: Stachys palustris subsp. ambigua, also known as hedge woundwort or marsh betony, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as betulinic acid and ursolic acid. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Stachys palustris subsp. ambigua is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
